nodes.label: P4.2 — depth headroom (A3) x difficulty
nodes.rationale: Depth-composition GENERALIZES across the whole difficulty band (not a P3.2 one-config fluke); OURS w=2 out-composes supervised BP in the mid/hard regime while BP wins easy — complementary regimes, crossover ~overlap 0.6.

nodes.label: A3 — depth-composition GENERALIZES across the whole difficulty band
nodes.rationale: OURS w=2 has a positive per-layer depth-slope (IQR disjoint from zero) at every overlap tested, overturning P3.2's fear that headroom composition was a one-config fluke; coordination (w=2 vs w=1) is the robust lever, not width.

nodes.label: Complementary depth-composition regimes: GD easy, OURS hard/mid
nodes.rationale: GD composes best where supervised gradient-headroom exists (easy); OURS out-composes GD in the mid/hard regime where BP's hidden-layer composition collapses (slope crosses negative by overlap 1.0) — crossover at top-layer readability ~overlap 0.6; supervised depth needs headroom, contrastive depth does not.

nodes.label: Coordination window size is a difficulty-dependent knob
nodes.rationale: w=2 is cheap and sufficient in the hard regime, but easy+deep configurations show an inverted-U (myopic drift past L4-5) that needs a longer coordination horizon (w=4, per P3.2) — flagged as a Phase-5 adaptive-window knob.
